creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
Running a 13.1-RELENG JAIL ontop of a 13.1-RELENG host serving an icingaweb2/icinga2-director frontend, the most recent icinga2-director module requires a dedicated icinga-director service to run and this is started via rc.d script.

In our case, on all jails running icinga2-director, this service doesn't startup at boot time of the jail. The jail's console log reports icinga-director has been started, but the service dies silently.

Login on the jail's console and starting icinga-director via "service ... start" works.

The reason seems to be Apache 2.4: service apache24 is started LATER than service icinga-director, in that case of a missing running apache24, icinga-direcotr won't start. If apache24 is already running, icinga-director also starts flawless.

How to reproduce: stop apache24 and icinga-director. Then try to start icinga-director without apache24 running. It will fail.

It seems the order of starting services is important and should somehow be guaranteed by the icinga-director rc script itself to assure apache24/php is running.
